name: Show-Markdown

options.name: -InputObject
options.description: A Markdown string that will be shown in the terminal. If you do not pass in a supported format, `Show-Markdown` will emit an error.

name: -LiteralPath
-PSPath
-LP
options.description: Specifies the path to a Markdown file. Unlike the Path parameter, the value of LiteralPath is used exactly as it is typed. No characters are interpreted as wildcards. If the path includes escape characters, enclose it in single quotation marks. Single quotation marks tell PowerShell not to interpret any characters as escape sequences.

options.name: -Path
options.description: Specifies the path to a Markdown file to be rendered.

options.name: -UseBrowser
options.description: Compiles the Markdown input as HTML and opens it in your default browser.

description: Shows a Markdown file or string in the console in a friendly way using VT100 escape sequences or in a browser using HTML.
